title: Heatwave grips 8 states including Rajasthan, MP; Akola records 44°C: Wardha shuts schools due to heat; snow... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Heatwave grips 8 states including Rajasthan, MP; Akola records 44°C:Wardha shuts schools due to heat; snowfall alert for J&K, Uttarakhand
New Delhi/Bhopal/Jaipur/Lucknow5 hours ago
Severe heat has begun affecting large parts of central and southern India. Over the past two days, temperatures have ranged between 40°C and 44°C across Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Gujarat, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Delhi, and Maharashtra.
On Wednesday, multiple regions recorded extreme temperatures-
Akola – 44.2°C
Barmer – 42.8°C
Nowgong – 42°C
Anantapur – 43.4°C
Rajnandgaon – 43°C
Banda – 44°C
Weak western disturbance behind sudden temperature rise
According to the India Meteorological Department, the primary reason for the sudden spike in temperatures is the weakening of western disturbances. Such conditions are expected to persist until 20 April, leading to severe heatwave conditions across western, central, and southern India.
Schools shut or timings revised due to the heat
To protect children from extreme heat, authorities in Wardha have ordered all schools to remain closed on Thursday. School timings have been revised in Burhanpur, Harda, and Ratlam
Weather forecast for the next 2 days
17 April
Light to moderate rainfall with strong winds likely in Punjab, Haryana, and Chandigarh. Light rain expected in Himachal Pradesh. Snowfall and hailstorms are possible in Jammu & Kashmir–Ladakh. Light rain with thunder and lightning likely in West Bengal and Odisha. Heatwave conditions will persist in Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, and Odisha.
18 April
Heatwave warning issued for Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Telangana, and Karnataka. Hot and humid conditions are expected in Gujarat, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal. Heavy rainfall likely in Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura
